items.title: Asked Keaton Wagler about the weirdest question he got during pre-draft:
items.author: Tomer Azarly
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Keaton Wagler about the weirdest question he got during pre-draft:

“1 team that put up pictures of me and my family, and a couple of them just weren't even my family. I was like, ‘that’s weird.’ They asked, ‘who are these people?’ and I was like, ‘I actually don't know who those people are.’"

items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
In tonight's second round of the 2026 NBA Draft, only four teams hold their original draft pick:

Orlando Magic - #46
Toronto Raptors - #50
Houston Rockets - #53
New York Knicks - #55

All 26 other second-round picks have changed hands at least once leading up to tonight.

items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
ICYMI:
One year ago, the Pacers were one win from the first NBA title in franchise history. Then Tyrese Haliburton went down in Game 7, and everything changed.

On what followed, the pain of the past year and the hope still ahead:
www.fieldhousefiles.com/p/pacers-tyr...

media.title: The Pacers’ storybook finish became a nightmare. One year later, hope remains.

items.title: Bulls should trade up in the second round tonight. Three targets: Bruce Thornton, Meleek Thomas, and Henri Veesaar. I'd put Isaiah Evans fourth. Bryson inherited a ton of seconds from AK's trade deadline purge. This is what they're for. Go get a third contributor from this draft.

items.title: Signing Reaves to a max would've taken some maneuvering for the Pistons.
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt:
Signing Reaves to a max would've taken some maneuvering for the Pistons. 

Cap space route would've been renouncing Harris, Huerter, Beasley, and Green in free agency, waiving Robinson and Reed, declining Tolu Smith's option and then trading either Stew or LeVert (likely Stew).
